title: Inotropes
body_html:
Dobutamine
  • Predominantly β1-adrenergic agonist (weak β2 and α1 activity)
  • Increases heart contractility and cardiac output
  • Less likely to cause hypertension than Dopamine as doesn't stimulate noradrenalin production

Dopamine
  • Causes increase in sodium excretion by the kidneys, an increase in urine output, an increase in heart rate, and an increase in blood pressure
  • Stimulates several dopamine receptors (which in turn increase noradrenalin)
  • Cardiovascular effects primarily result of dopamine acting at α1, β1, and β2 adrenergic receptors

Cardiovascular pharmacology

  • Less likely to cause hypertension than Dopamine as doesn't stimulate noradrenalin production

  • Stimulates several dopamine receptors (which in turn increase noradrenalin)
  • Cardiovascular effects primarily result of dopamine acting at α1, β1, and β2 adrenergic receptors
